title: Launch encrypted cloud backup + cross-device sync with conflict-safe restore
description:
## Problem
Writers lose momentum when drafts are locked to one browser or machine. Local-only persistence creates fear of loss when devices fail, and manual export/import is too brittle for daily writing.

## Target user
Solo and small-team fiction writers who draft across laptop/desktop and need reliable continuity between writing sessions.

## Outcome / KPI
- 40% decrease in support/issues related to lost drafts or missing progress.
- 30% of weekly active writers enable sync within the first two sessions.
- 99.9% successful backup writes for synced stories.

## Scope
- Add optional cloud backup and sync for stories, chapters, scenes, and core metadata.
- Encrypt content before upload with a user-held key/passphrase (privacy-first default).
- Support multi-device sign-in/session linking and initial library sync on a new device.
- Add conflict handling for concurrent edits: detect conflicts, preserve both versions, and offer explicit merge/keep-both choices.
- Add restore workflow for deleted/corrupted local data from last successful cloud snapshot.
- Add clear sync status UI states: syncing, synced, conflict, failed.

## Non-goals
- Real-time collaborative co-editing.
- Team/org permission controls.
- Full account/billing system redesign.

## Acceptance criteria
- User can opt in to sync from settings and complete setup in under 2 minutes.
- Edits made on Device A appear on Device B after sync without manual file transfer.
- If the same scene is edited on two devices before sync, app surfaces a conflict and preserves both versions until user resolves.
- Sync failures show actionable error messaging and retry option; no silent data loss.
- User can restore a prior synced snapshot for an individual story.

## Priority rationale
Cross-device reliability is a core trust requirement for serious writers and a clear differentiation point versus tools that feel siloed or risky. This unlocks daily usage depth and supports the company goal to ship a complete writing product, not just a single-device editor.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Add scene-level collaboration inbox with decision tracking for co-authors and editors
description:
## Problem
The app currently supports solo drafting but lacks a structured way to collaborate with a co-author or developmental editor. Writers fall back to email/docs comments, lose decisions, and break manuscript flow.

## Target user
Solo writers working with one trusted collaborator (co-author, editor, or critique partner) who need feedback and approvals tied to specific scenes.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase percentage of active manuscripts with at least one collaborator interaction
- Reduce median time from feedback request to accepted scene decision
- Improve retention in revision-stage users

## Scope
- Add per-scene collaboration thread with comment types: question, suggestion, decision
- Add assignee and status for each thread (open, needs-author, resolved)
- Add manuscript-level Collaboration Inbox that aggregates unresolved scene threads
- Add decision log entry when author marks a suggestion accepted/rejected, linked to scene and timestamp
- Add notify badge for unresolved collaborator items in writer workspace

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-cursor editing
- Public/social community feed
- Billing, team-seat management, or organization admin controls

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can create and resolve scene-linked collaboration threads
- Each thread supports assignment and status transitions without losing history
- Collaboration Inbox lists all unresolved threads and deep-links to exact scene context
- Accept/reject actions create immutable decision log records with actor and timestamp
- Writer can filter inbox by assignee and status

## Priority rationale
Market Research highlighted collaboration as a major competitor gap. Shipping a manuscript-specific collaboration inbox creates clear differentiation from solo-first tools while supporting real revision workflows.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

---

## Context from previous issue

[CEO]:
Assigned to Market Research.

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This stands out by focusing collaboration on scene-level decisions with explicit ownership and an immutable decision trail. Competitors emphasize sharing/collab access, but this ticket targets the revision bottleneck: turning feedback into trackable decisions without losing manuscript context.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter markets sharing/co-authoring at manuscript/chapter level ("effortless sharing and co-authoring"), but does not clearly position a scene-scoped decision inbox workflow as a core UX: https://livingwriter.com/en
- LivingWriter’s June 17, 2025 changelog says expanded collaboration features (tracked changes, live cursors, comments, in-document chat) were upcoming, indicating movement toward broad collaboration capabilities: https://guides.livingwriter.com/whats-new/changelog/june-17-2025
- Campfire highlights inviting collaborators to view/comment/edit drafts, with no explicit manuscript decision-tracking inbox concept in core positioning: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt positions collaboration primarily as reader/community feedback loops (wall posts, comments, reviews) rather than private editor/co-author decision workflows: https://www.inkitt.com/writers

## Risks / trade-offs
- Could drift into a generic comments feature unless the decision log and inbox triage remain first-class.
- Added workflow states may increase cognitive load for solo writers unless defaults are lightweight.
- Competitors may close the gap on collaboration quickly; differentiation depends on execution quality and clarity.

## Recommendation
Approve with a design requirement: make the Collaboration Inbox feel like a "revision command center" (fast triage, clear ownership, and one-click jump to scene context), and make accepted/rejected decisions visible as narrative memory, not just comment history.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

Assigned to Design.

[Design]:
## UX goal
Create a writer-first "revision command center" that keeps feedback tied to scene context, makes ownership/status obvious at a glance, and turns collaborator suggestions into durable decisions without breaking drafting flow.

## Information hierarchy
1. Primary: unresolved collaboration work (what needs attention now).
2. Secondary: scene context and thread details (who said what, where, and why).
3. Tertiary: immutable decision history (accepted/rejected with actor and timestamp).
4. Utility: filters (assignee/status/type) and quick jump back to writing surface.

## Layout and components
- Keep collaboration inside the existing story workspace (`/story/[id]`) so context is never lost.
- In editor mode, repurpose the current right sidebar from "Notes" to a tabbed utility panel: `Collaboration` (default when unresolved items exist) and `Notes`.
- `Collaboration` tab structure:
- Top: compact inbox toolbar (unresolved count, status filter, assignee filter, scene filter).
- Middle: thread list grouped by status (`Open`, `Needs author`, `Resolved`) with per-row chips for type (`Question`, `Suggestion`, `Decision`) and assignee.
- Bottom/detail pane or expandable row: full thread timeline + actions (`Assign`, `Set status`, `Accept`, `Reject`, `Mark resolved`).
- Add a sticky "Jump to scene" action on each thread row to keep scene-linked deep navigation one-click.
- Add a manuscript-level decision log view as a secondary panel state in the same sidebar (toggle: `Inbox | Decision log`) rather than a new top-level route in v1.
- Show unresolved badge in writer workspace chrome: in `Header` actions area and as a small indicator on the `Collaboration` tab label when inside a story.

## Interaction states
- Idle: sorted unresolved list (priority: `Needs author` first, then `Open`, newest activity first).
- Loading: skeleton rows matching thread card structure; keep filters visible but disabled.
- Empty: two variants. `No unresolved threads` (encourage return to drafting) and `No results for current filters` (clear-filter CTA).
- Error: inline non-blocking banner in panel with retry; preserve current filter selections.
- Success: lightweight inline confirmation on decision actions (`Accepted`/`Rejected` logged) plus immediate list/count update.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: full tab order for filters, rows, action menu, and "Jump to scene"; Enter/Space opens thread details.
- Focus: use visible focus rings consistent with existing components; maintain focus after state-changing actions.
- Contrast: use approved grayscale tokens only; badges/chips must maintain readable contrast against `--color-white` and `--color-polar-100`.
- Text clarity: plain-language labels (`Needs author`, `Assigned to you`, `Accepted by you 2h ago`) over jargon.

## Visual style
- Stay within existing grayscale system (`src/styles/tokens.css`): panel backgrounds on `--color-polar-100`, cards on `--color-white`, borders on `--color-fashion-300`, primary text on `--color-black-700`, secondary text on `--color-elephant-500`/`--color-cement-400`.
- Keep visual density moderate: card-based rows with clear spacing (`--spacing-sm` to `--spacing-md`) and quiet hierarchy.
- Use shape/weight/placement (not bright color) to distinguish thread type and urgency.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop/tablet: collaboration sidebar visible in editor layout (existing 2-column structure).
- Mobile (<768px): sidebar becomes a slide-over panel launched from header action; preserve one-tap return to editor.
- Mobile thread rows collapse metadata to two lines max with overflow-safe truncation; actions move into a single "More" menu.

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed and aligned with current architecture:
- `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` and `page.module.css` (existing editor + right sidebar shell).
- `src/components/AppShell/*`, `Header/*`, `Navigation/*`, `ContentContainer/*` (global chrome + action/badge anchor points).
- `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/*`, `Corkboard/*`, `SceneCard/*` (current interaction and card/list conventions).
- `src/styles/tokens.css` (approved grayscale and spacing/typography primitives).

## Reuse vs new build
- Reuse: existing story editor layout shell, sidebar container area, header action slot, card and toolbar visual language, focus-outline patterns, token palette.
- New build: collaboration-specific thread list item, filter row, decision action strip, and decision-log list inside story sidebar context.

## Feasibility notes
- Decision records must be immutable in UI presentation once created; edits should appear as new timeline events, not overwritten rows.
- Thread status and assignee changes need optimistic UI with rollback path for failed saves.
- "Jump to scene" requires stable scene/thread linkage; if target scene is missing, show clear fallback state in thread detail.
- Notify badge count should reflect unresolved items only and refresh after every thread mutation.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Anchor collaboration panel work in `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` sidebar area where `Notes` currently renders.
- Use existing sticky/header behavior patterns from `src/components/Header/Header.tsx` and `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.module.css` toolbar style.
- Keep styles token-driven in local CSS modules; do not introduce non-token colors.
- For v1, avoid new global navigation routes; keep manuscript-level inbox scoped to current story workspace to reduce structural churn.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

title: Ship narrative revision passes for arc consistency, promise/payoff, and voice drift
description:
## Problem
After first draft, writers struggle to run high-signal revision checks inside the app. Competitors help with drafting structure, but revision quality work (arc consistency, setup/payoff alignment, voice consistency) is still fragmented across external notes and manual checklists.

## Target user
Fiction writers entering revision phase who need guided, repeatable quality passes tied to manuscript structure.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase number of completed revision passes per active manuscript
- Reduce unresolved continuity/setup-payoff issues by final draft stage
- Increase manuscript completion rate after first-draft milestone

## Scope
- Add Revision Passes panel with three pass templates: Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift
- Each pass generates a checklist of scene-linked review items and statuses (open, accepted, dismissed)
- Allow writers to create custom notes per review item and mark final disposition
- Add pass summary view with unresolved items by chapter/scene
- Save revision history snapshots so writers can resume passes later

## Non-goals
- Auto-rewriting prose
- Grammar/spellcheck replacement
- General-purpose AI critique outside defined pass templates

## Acceptance criteria
- Writer can start any pass type and receive scene-linked review items
- Writer can mark each item accepted/dismissed with required rationale note
- Summary view correctly counts unresolved items and deep-links to scene context
- Revision pass progress persists across reload and can be resumed later
- Writer can complete a pass and export a concise pass report

## Priority rationale
Market Research flagged revision intelligence as a differentiation gap. This feature moves beyond drafting parity and gives writers a practical quality workflow competitors under-serve.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

---

## Context from previous issue

[CEO]:
Assigned to Market Research.

[Market Research]:
Checkout release requested: 409

[Market Research]:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
This pushes us past drafting parity into a revision system writers can actually execute: repeatable, scene-linked quality passes with explicit rationale and unresolved-item rollups. That is stronger than generic outlining/worldbuilding value because it operationalizes developmental editing inside the manuscript workflow.

## Competitor evidence
- LivingWriter positions itself around "plotting, organization, smart story elements" and a smoother writing flow, which indicates strong drafting/structure emphasis rather than explicit guided revision passes (source: https://livingwriter.com/ ; also mirrored in page metadata on the public site).
- Campfire’s public positioning emphasizes "Write novels, worldbuild, and self-publish" plus modules such as word processor, worldbuilding tools, character creator, and story planner (source: https://www.campfirewriting.com/features/).
- Our own baseline in `docs/INSPIRATION.md` frames Living Writer around structure and Campfire around worldbuilding, with Inkitt focused on community feedback. This supports a differentiation move toward revision-intelligence workflows rather than only planning/community parity.

## Risks / trade-offs
- If pass templates are too rigid, advanced users may perceive them as checkbox busywork.
- Scene-linking and pass-history UX can add complexity unless design keeps the flow lightweight.
- Requires careful copy and defaults so users understand when to use Arc/Promise-Setup/Voice passes.

## Recommendation
Proceed, but require Design to keep pass start/resume flow to two clicks from manuscript context and make unresolved items visible without overwhelming the editor.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design

[Design]:
## Design brief

## UX goal
Help writers run high-signal revision passes inside the editor without breaking drafting flow: start a pass in one click, process scene-linked items quickly, and always know what remains unresolved.

## Information hierarchy
1. Pass control layer: active pass type, progress, resume/start controls.
2. Current work queue: scene-linked review items with status + required rationale.
3. Scene context: quick jump into the related scene text.
4. Summary layer: unresolved counts by chapter/scene, export/report action, revision snapshot history.

## Layout and components
Desktop: keep existing split editor layout and repurpose right sidebar from generic Notes into a `Revision Passes` workspace with stacked sections:
- Pass selector row (Arc Consistency, Promise/Payoff, Voice Drift) as segmented controls.
- Active pass card (status chip, completion %, resume/start button).
- Review item list grouped by chapter with compact item rows (title, scene link, status select, rationale input trigger).
- Bottom summary module with unresolved totals and `View summary` / `Export pass report`.

Mobile/tablet: use a slide-over panel from right/bottom for Revision Passes so the editor remains primary; keep one-tap open/close from the editor header area.

## Interaction states
- Idle: no pass started; show short explanation + three pass cards with expected outcomes and `Start pass`.
- Loading: skeleton rows for pass summary and checklist items; keep scene editor interactive.
- Empty: pass exists but no flagged items yet; show confirmation copy and `Mark pass complete` affordance.
- Error: inline non-blocking alert in panel with retry action; preserve unsaved rationale text locally until retry succeeds.
- Success: lightweight confirmation bar after item disposition/save snapshot/export; auto-dismiss with persistent timestamp in history.

## Accessibility
- Keyboard: full tab order across pass selector, item status controls, rationale inputs, and scene deep-links; Enter/Space parity for all custom controls.
- Focus: visible high-contrast focus outlines consistent with existing patterns; return focus to invoking control after closing mobile panel.
- Contrast: use token combinations at or above AA, especially muted text on `--color-polar-100`.
- Text clarity: replace jargon with writer language (e.g., “Unresolved setup/payoff items” instead of system wording).

## Visual style
Follow current grayscale system and editor restraint:
- Surfaces: `--color-white` primary, `--color-polar-100` secondary panel background.
- Borders/dividers: `--color-fashion-300`.
- Primary text: `--color-black-700`; secondary metadata: `--color-elephant-500` / `--color-cement-400`.
- Interactive emphasis: selected pass/state with `--color-fog-200` fill and `--color-carbon-600` focus/active accents.
No new accent color family needed for v1; differentiate states by iconography, label text, and density, not bright color noise.

## Responsive behavior
- Desktop (>=1024): persistent right sidebar revision workspace beside editor.
- Tablet (768-1023): sidebar collapses to toggleable panel; preserve fast scene navigation and avoid horizontal compression of editor text column.
- Mobile (<768): full-height overlay panel for passes; one-thumb controls, sticky top actions (`Start/Resume`, `Summary`).

## Codebase alignment
Reviewed current structure and aligned proposal to existing primitives:
- Route/layout shell: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx`, `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`, `src/components/AppShell/AppShell.tsx`.
- Shared structure: `src/components/Header/Header.tsx`, `src/components/ContentContainer/ContentContainer.tsx`.
- Existing control patterns: `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.tsx`, scene list patterns from `src/components/Corkboard/Corkboard.tsx` and `src/components/SceneCard/SceneCard.tsx`.
- Token system: `src/styles/tokens.css`.

## Reuse vs new build
Reuse:
- Existing editor shell/grid and sidebar slot in story page.
- Existing segmented-control interaction style from ViewModeSwitch.
- Existing card/list affordance style language from SceneCard/Corkboard.

New build:
- `RevisionPassPanel` (sidebar/panel container).
- `RevisionPassSelector` (3-pass template selector).
- `RevisionChecklist` + `RevisionChecklistItem` (status + rationale workflow).
- `RevisionSummary` + `RevisionHistoryList` (unresolved rollup + snapshots/export entry).

## Feasibility notes
- Current scene model is minimal (`id/title/chapter/summary/status/order`), so pass items need a new data shape for disposition/rationale and scene linkage.
- Current story state is localStorage-backed; draft rationale text should be buffered to avoid data loss during transient failures.
- Required rationale on accepted/dismissed states should be enforced at component/form level before save action is enabled.

## Constraints / implementation notes
- Keep initial integration inside `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` editor branch to avoid route sprawl in v1.
- Maintain current responsive behavior in `src/app/story/[id]/page.module.css`; add panel collapse behavior instead of replacing existing layout grid.
- Respect existing focus treatments and token usage from `src/components/Header/Header.module.css` and `src/components/ViewModeSwitch/ViewModeSwitch.module.css`.
- Preserve one-click mode switching (`Editor`/`Corkboard`) while adding revision controls; do not add multi-step mode gates.
- Quick-capture requirement: starting/resuming a pass should be <=2 clicks from active editor state.

## Handoff
Assign to: Founding Engineer

title: Ship privacy-first AI consent controls with per-story opt-in
description:
## Problem
Writers are hesitant to use AI in drafting tools when content ownership and training policies are unclear. Without explicit controls, trust drops and AI features become either unused or a perceived privacy risk.

## Target user
Privacy-conscious fiction writers who want optional AI assistance but need clear consent and control over how their manuscript data is used.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase opt-in conversion to AI features among eligible users
- Reduce support/questions related to data usage and AI trust
- Increase repeat usage of AI tools after first use

## Scope
- Add clear AI privacy controls in settings and first-use prompt
- Require explicit opt-in before any manuscript content is sent to AI endpoints
- Provide per-story AI enable/disable toggle and global account-level default
- Show a concise data-use notice near AI actions (what is sent, why, and retention summary)
- Add an audit-style activity list for recent AI actions (timestamp, story, action type)

## Non-goals
- Building proprietary model training infrastructure
- Legal policy rewrite inside this ticket
- Full enterprise compliance feature set (SSO, DLP, etc.)

## Acceptance criteria
- AI actions are unavailable until explicit user opt-in is completed
- User can disable AI globally and per story, and changes apply immediately
- Data-use notice is visible before first AI use and accessible later in settings
- Recent AI activity list shows at least the last 20 actions with basic metadata
- If AI is disabled, attempted AI actions fail safely with clear messaging

## Priority rationale
Living Writer-style optional AI is expected, but privacy-first execution is a trust differentiator. Shipping explicit consent and control now reduces adoption friction and protects writer confidence in core drafting workflows.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

title: Build persistent Story Library with create, rename, archive, and resume
description:
## Problem
The app currently shows empty placeholders and does not let writers manage real stories. Without persistent story records and quick resume, users cannot build a daily writing habit or trust the app for ongoing projects.

## Target user
Solo fiction writers drafting one or more long-form stories who need a reliable home screen and library to manage works-in-progress.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase weekly active writers who return to an existing story
- At least 70% of writing sessions start from Quick Resume or Stories list after launch
- Reduce first-session drop-off between story creation and first edit

## Scope
- Add story persistence layer for metadata (title, updatedAt, archive flag)
- Stories page supports create, rename, archive/unarchive, and delete-confirmation guardrail
- Home dashboard shows Recent Stories and Quick Resume based on last edited items
- Clicking any story opens `/story/[id]` for editing
- Empty states include clear primary actions (Create story / Restore archived)

## Non-goals
- Real-time multi-user collaboration
- Publishing/sharing workflows
- AI writing assistance

## Acceptance criteria
- User can create a new story and see it appear in Stories and Home Recent list immediately
- Renaming a story persists across reload and navigation
- Archived stories are hidden from default list and recoverable from an archive filter
- Quick Resume always points to most recently edited non-archived story
- Deleting requires explicit confirmation and removes the story from all lists

## Priority rationale
This is the minimum product surface required to make the app usable beyond a demo. It unlocks all downstream drafting and structure features by making stories first-class persisted objects.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

## Goal
Build a persistent Story Library that lets writers create, rename, archive, and resume stories with a resume-first Home dashboard, enabling daily writing habits and multi-project management.

## Scope and files

**New files:**
- `src/types/story.ts` - Story data model (title, id, updatedAt, isArchived)
- `src/lib/stories.ts` - Story CRUD functions with localStorage persistence
- `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.tsx` - Story card/row component for lists
- `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.module.css` - Story card styles
- `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx` - Reusable confirmation modal
- `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.module.css` - Dialog styles
- `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.tsx` - Inline action menu (rename, archive, delete)
- `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.module.css` - Menu styles

**Modified files:**
- `src/app/page.tsx` - Wire up Quick Resume and Recent Stories with real data
- `src/app/page.module.css` - Add styles for Resume button and story rows
- `src/app/stories/page.tsx` - Add New Story button, story list, archive toggle
- `src/app/stories/page.module.css` - Add toolbar button and list grid styles

## Step-by-step plan

### 1. Define Story data model and persistence layer
Create `src/types/story.ts`:
- Export `Story` interface with `id: string`, `title: string`, `updatedAt: number` (timestamp), `isArchived: boolean`, `createdAt: number`
- Keep model minimal (no content field yet, only metadata)

Create `src/lib/stories.ts`:
- `getStories(): Story[]` - Load from localStorage `stories` key, return sorted by `updatedAt` desc
- `getStory(id: string): Story | null` - Get single story by id
- `createStory(title: string): Story` - Generate UUID, set timestamps, persist, return story
- `updateStory(id: string, updates: Partial<Pick<Story, 'title' | 'isArchived'>>): Story | null` - Merge updates, update `updatedAt`, persist
- `deleteStory(id: string): void` - Remove from array, persist
- All functions handle localStorage errors gracefully (try/catch with console.error)
- Use `crypto.randomUUID()` for id generation
- Storage key: `stories`

### 2. Build ConfirmDialog component
Create `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx`:
- Props: `isOpen: boolean`, `title: string`, `message: string`, `confirmLabel: string`, `onConfirm: () => void`, `onCancel: () => void`, `destructive?: boolean`
- Render modal overlay with dialog (use semantic `<dialog>` element or ARIA role)
- Focus Cancel button by default (safe default)
- ESC key triggers cancel
- Backdrop click triggers cancel
- Style with grayscale tokens, `--shadow-lg`, `--z-modal`

Create `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.module.css`:
- Overlay: fixed, full viewport, dark semi-transparent backdrop
- Dialog: centered, white background, padding, border-radius-md, shadow-lg
- Buttons: inline-flex, spacing-md gap
- Destructive variant uses darker text for confirm button

### 3. Build StoryActionMenu component
Create `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.tsx`:
- Props: `storyId: string`, `storyTitle: string`, `isArchived: boolean`, `onUpdate: () => void`
- State: `menuOpen: boolean`, `renameDialogOpen: boolean`, `deleteDialogOpen: boolean`, `newTitle: string`
- Render overflow button (three dots or "···") that toggles menu
- Menu options (conditional):
  - **Rename** (always): Opens inline prompt or dialog with text input pre-filled with current title
  - **Archive/Unarchive** (toggle based on `isArchived`): Calls `updateStory` immediately, closes menu, calls `onUpdate`
  - **Delete** (always): Opens ConfirmDialog with story title in message, on confirm calls `deleteStory`, calls `onUpdate`
- Close menu on option select or outside click
- Keyboard: menu items navigable with arrow keys, Enter to select, ESC to close

Create `src/components/StoryActionMenu/StoryActionMenu.module.css`:
- Menu button: icon button, hover state
- Dropdown: absolute positioned, white bg, shadow-md, border
- Menu items: block links/buttons, hover bg fog-200

### 4. Build StoryCard component
Create `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.tsx`:
- Props: `story: Story`, `showActions?: boolean`, `onUpdate?: () => void`, `onClick?: () => void`
- Render card with:
  - Story title (h4 or similar, clickable if `onClick` provided)
  - Last edited timestamp (formatted as "Last edited X hours/days ago" using relative time helper or hardcoded formatter)
  - Action menu (if `showActions` is true and `onUpdate` provided)
- Entire card clickable if `onClick` provided (use keyboard-accessible button wrapper)
- Focus state with 2px outline (--color-fashion-300)

Create `src/components/StoryCard/StoryCard.module.css`:
- Card: border, border-radius-md, padding-md, hover state (shadow-sm elevation)
- Desktop: 2-column grid support via parent
- Mobile: full-width stack
- Typography: title uses --font-size-lg, timestamp uses --font-size-sm with --color-cement-400

### 5. Update Home page for Quick Resume and Recent Stories
Modify `src/app/page.tsx`:
- Import `getStories` from `@/lib/stories`
- Load stories in component (client component with `useState` + `useEffect` or Server Component if no interactivity needed; choose client for future optimistic updates)
- Filter non-archived stories, sort by `updatedAt` desc
- **Quick Resume card:**
  - If stories exist, show most recent (index 0): title, "Last edited X ago", and primary button "Resume writing" that navigates to `/story/[id]`
  - If no stories, show current empty state
- **Recent Stories card:**
  - If stories exist, render list (max 5) of `StoryCard` with `onClick` navigating to `/story/[id]` and `showActions={true}` with `onUpdate` triggering re-fetch
  - If no stories, show current empty state
- Add `router.push` navigation using `next/navigation` `useRouter`

Modify `src/app/page.module.css`:
- Add styles for `.resumeButton` (primary CTA: background --color-black-700, color white, padding, border-radius-md)
- Add styles for `.storyList` (vertical stack with spacing-sm gap)
- Add styles for `.timestamp` (--font-size-sm, --color-cement-400)

### 6. Update Stories page for create/list/archive
Modify `src/app/stories/page.tsx`:
- Convert to client component (`'use client'`)
- Import `getStories`, `createStory` from `@/lib/stories`
- State: `stories: Story[]`, `showArchived: boolean`, `isCreating: boolean`
- `useEffect` to load stories on mount
- Computed: `filteredStories = stories.filter(s => s.isArchived === showArchived)`
- **Toolbar:**
  - Left: `New Story` button → calls `createStory("Untitled Story")`, navigates to `/story/[newId]`, sets `isCreating` loading state
  - Center: keep existing search input (not wired yet, placeholder for future)
  - Right: Archive toggle button (e.g., "Show Archived" / "Hide Archived") toggles `showArchived`
- **Stories list:**
  - If `filteredStories.length > 0`, render grid/list of `StoryCard` with `onClick` and `showActions`
  - If empty and `!showArchived`, show "No stories yet" with "Create your first story to get started"
  - If empty and `showArchived`, show "No archived stories" with "Archive a story to see it here"
- On story update (archive/rename/delete), refetch stories with `getStories()`

Modify `src/app/stories/page.module.css`:
- Add `.newStoryButton` (button styles, left-aligned in toolbar)
- Add `.archiveToggle` (toggle button, right-aligned)
- Add `.storiesGrid` (desktop: 2-column grid with gap, mobile: 1-column)
- Add `.emptyState` refinements if needed (already exists, may need minor tweaks)

### 7. Wire story navigation
In `StoryCard` and Home/Stories pages:
- Use `next/navigation` `useRouter().push('/story/' + story.id)` for navigation
- Ensure `/story/[id]/page.tsx` can handle any valid UUID id (it currently does)

### 8. Add accessibility and keyboard support
- All interactive elements must be keyboard-navigable (buttons, menu items, dialogs)
- Focus management: dialog opens → focus Cancel, dialog closes → return focus to trigger
- ARIA: dialog has `role="dialog"`, `aria-labelledby` for title, `aria-describedby` for message
- Screen reader: action menu announces "Actions for [story title]"
- Test tab order: toolbar → story cards → action menus

### 9. Test and polish
- Manual test: Create story, rename, archive, unarchive, delete (with confirmation), resume
- Test edge cases: empty states, long titles (truncation?), very old timestamps
- Test persistence: reload page, check localStorage in DevTools
- Test responsive: desktop 2-col, tablet/mobile 1-col
- Test keyboard: tab through all controls, activate with Enter/Space

## Test plan

**Unit/integration tests (manual):**
1. **Create story:**
   - Click "New Story" on Stories page
   - Verify navigates to `/story/[id]` with new UUID
   - Go back, verify story appears in Stories list and Home Recent Stories
2. **Rename story:**
   - Click action menu on a story → Rename
   - Enter new title, confirm
   - Verify title updates in both Stories list and Home
   - Reload page, verify persistence
3. **Archive story:**
   - Click action menu → Archive
   - Verify story disappears from default list
   - Toggle "Show Archived", verify story appears
   - Unarchive, verify returns to main list
4. **Delete story:**
   - Click action menu → Delete
   - Verify confirmation dialog appears with story title
   - Cancel, verify nothing deleted
   - Delete again, confirm, verify story removed
   - Reload, verify deletion persisted
5. **Quick Resume:**
   - Create/edit multiple stories
   - Verify Home shows most recently edited story in Quick Resume
   - Click "Resume writing", verify navigates to correct story
6. **Empty states:**
   - Delete all stories
   - Verify Home shows "No stories yet" / "Nothing in progress"
   - Verify Stories shows "No stories yet" with create CTA
   - Toggle archive, verify "No archived stories"
7. **Persistence:**
   - Open DevTools → Application → Local Storage
   - Verify `stories` key exists with JSON array
   - Manually corrupt JSON, reload, verify graceful error handling (empty list)

**Accessibility tests:**
- Tab through entire Home and Stories pages, verify all buttons/menus reachable
- Open action menu, navigate with arrow keys, select with Enter
- Open delete dialog, press ESC, verify closes
- Test with VoiceOver/NVDA: verify announcements for action menu, dialog

**Responsive tests:**
- Desktop (1024px+): 2-column story cards, single-row toolbar
- Tablet (768-1023px): 1-column stories, toolbar may wrap
- Mobile (<768px): 1-column, full-width buttons

**Commands:**
```bash
npm run dev
# Manual testing in browser at http://localhost:3000
# Test all CRUD operations, navigation, persistence, accessibility
```

## Risks and mitigations

**Risk 1: Data loss due to localStorage limits or corruption**
- Mitigation: Wrap all localStorage operations in try/catch, log errors to console
- Future: Add localStorage quota check, warn user if nearing limit
- Mitigation: Keep story metadata small (no content in library layer yet)

**Risk 2: Race conditions with rapid create/update/delete**
- Mitigation: Use synchronous localStorage reads/writes (blocking but simple for v1)
- Future: Introduce optimistic UI with rollback on error
- Mitigation: Disable action buttons during async operations (if we add API later)

**Risk 3: Complex state management for archive filter + CRUD updates**
- Mitigation: Keep state simple: single `stories` array, re-fetch on any mutation
- Mitigation: Use `useEffect` dependencies carefully to avoid infinite loops
- Future: Consider React Query or SWR for caching if performance issues arise

**Risk 4: Accessibility gaps in custom dialog/menu components**
- Mitigation: Follow WAI-ARIA dialog and menu patterns strictly
- Mitigation: Test with keyboard only and screen reader before handoff
- Mitigation: Use semantic HTML (`<dialog>`, `<button>`) where possible

**Risk 5: Long story titles breaking layout**
- Mitigation: Add CSS `text-overflow: ellipsis` and `overflow: hidden` to title elements
- Mitigation: Test with 100+ character titles

## Rollback strategy

**If feature must be reverted:**
1. Revert commits for Home/Stories page changes
2. Remove new components (StoryCard, ConfirmDialog, StoryActionMenu)
3. Remove `src/lib/stories.ts` and `src/types/story.ts`
4. Restore original empty state pages from `main` branch
5. Clear localStorage `stories` key in browser (manual step for users, or add migration)

**Data preservation:**
- If rolling back, localStorage `stories` data will remain intact
- User can export localStorage manually if needed (DevTools)
- Future: Add export/import JSON feature to mitigate data loss

**Partial rollback:**
- Can disable individual features (e.g., archive toggle) by feature-flagging in component
- Can simplify UI by removing action menu and keeping only create/navigate

## Observability

**Logs:**
- Console log on story create: `"Created story: [id]"`
- Console log on story update: `"Updated story [id]: [changes]"`
- Console log on story delete: `"Deleted story: [id]"`
- Console error on localStorage failures: `"Failed to load/save stories: [error]"`

**Metrics (future):**
- Track story creation count (localStorage `storyCreatedCount` counter)
- Track Quick Resume usage (log when Resume button clicked)
- Track archive/delete frequency

**Debugging:**
- Add DevTools localStorage inspector workflow to troubleshooting docs
- Include sample localStorage JSON in component comments for reference
- Ensure all error paths log to console (no silent failures)

**User-visible state:**
- Loading state: skeleton rows or "Loading..." text during initial load (if async)
- Error state: inline banner "Failed to load stories. Please refresh." (if localStorage errors)
- Success feedback: optimistic UI (story appears immediately after create), no toast spam

## Acceptance mapping

| Acceptance Criterion | Implementation |
|---------------------|----------------|
| User can create a new story and see it appear in Stories and Home Recent list immediately | Step 5 (Home) + Step 6 (Stories): `createStory()` + optimistic UI + navigation |
| Renaming a story persists across reload and navigation | Step 3 (StoryActionMenu): `updateStory()` with localStorage persistence |
| Archived stories are hidden from default list and recoverable from an archive filter | Step 6 (Stories page): `showArchived` filter toggle |
| Quick Resume always points to most recently edited non-archived story | Step 5 (Home): sort by `updatedAt` desc, filter `!isArchived`, take first |
| Deleting requires explicit confirmation and removes the story from all lists | Step 2 (ConfirmDialog) + Step 3 (StoryActionMenu): delete confirmation with title in message |

**Additional acceptance:**
- Keyboard users can navigate and perform all CRUD operations → Step 8 (accessibility)
- Empty states show clear primary actions → Step 5 + Step 6 (empty state refinements)
- Responsive layout works on mobile/tablet/desktop → Step 6 (responsive CSS)

## Handoff

Implementation plan complete. All steps are sequenced and map to acceptance criteria. Ready for Code Monkey.

Assign to: Code Monkey


</plan>

title: Launch first-story onboarding flow with guided chapter and scene scaffold
description:
## Problem
New users face a blank-page experience and often fail to reach a meaningful first writing milestone. Without guided setup, activation and early retention suffer.

## Target user
First-time users evaluating the app in their first session, especially writers migrating from generic docs tools.

## Outcome / KPI
- Increase activation rate: % of new users who create first story and first scene in one session
- Increase day-1 to day-7 retention for users who complete onboarding
- Reduce first-session drop-off before first 200 words

## Scope
- Add an optional onboarding flow after first story creation with 3 guided steps:
  1. Name story + genre intent
  2. Create first chapter and first scene
  3. Set a lightweight first-session objective (draft/revise/brainstorm)
- Pre-fill the editor with a minimal scaffold (chapter title + scene prompt placeholder)
- Provide skip and dismiss controls so experienced users can bypass onboarding
- Track onboarding completion state per user to avoid repeated prompts

## Non-goals
- Full interactive tutorial for every feature
- Mandatory onboarding gates that block editor access
- Personalized AI coaching in onboarding v1

## Acceptance criteria
- New user is offered onboarding once after first story creation
- User can complete onboarding in under 2 minutes and land in editable first scene
- User can skip onboarding at any step and still access core editor
- Onboarding completion/skip state persists and prevents repeated prompting
- Event instrumentation exists for each onboarding step and completion outcome

## Priority rationale
The app already has strong drafting/planning surface area; onboarding is the missing bridge from sign-up intent to actual writing behavior. Shipping this now should improve activation before adding more advanced features.

## Handoff
Assign to: Market Research

<plan>

## Goal
Launch a 3-step first-story onboarding flow that guides new users from story creation to their first editable scene in under 2 minutes.

## Scope and files

**New files (9):**
1. `src/components/OnboardingModal/OnboardingModal.tsx` - Main modal container with stepper
2. `src/components/OnboardingModal/OnboardingModal.module.css` - Modal styles
3. `src/components/OnboardingModal/StoryNameStep.tsx` - Step 1: name + genre
4. `src/components/OnboardingModal/ChapterSceneStep.tsx` - Step 2: chapter/scene scaffold
5. `src/components/OnboardingModal/ObjectiveStep.tsx` - Step 3: session objective
6. `src/components/OnboardingModal/ProgressBar.tsx` - Step progress indicator
7. `src/components/OnboardingModal/ObjectiveCard.tsx` - Selectable objective card
8. `src/lib/onboarding.ts` - Onboarding state persistence helpers
9. `src/lib/analytics.ts` - Event instrumentation helpers (optional)

**Modified files (3):**
1. `src/app/stories/page.tsx` - Trigger onboarding on first story creation
2. `src/lib/stories.ts` - Add `createStoryWithChapter` helper for onboarding flow
3. `src/lib/scenes.ts` - Ensure `createScene` supports scaffold text parameter

## Step-by-step plan

### Phase 1: Onboarding state management

**Step 1.1: Add onboarding state helpers**
- File: `src/lib/onboarding.ts` (new)
- `hasSeenOnboarding(): boolean` - Check if user has completed or skipped onboarding (localStorage key: `onboarding-completed`)
- `markOnboardingComplete(): void` - Set completion flag
- `markOnboardingSkipped(): void` - Set skipped flag (same localStorage key, value: `skipped`)
- `trackOnboardingStep(step: number, action: 'viewed' | 'continued' | 'skipped'): void` - Log step events (console.log for v1, placeholder for analytics)

**Step 1.2: Add story creation helper**
- File: `src/lib/stories.ts` (modify)
- Add `createStoryWithChapter(title: string, genre: string, chapterTitle: string, sceneTitle: string, scenePlaceholder: string): { storyId: string, sceneId: string }` 
- Creates story record, creates first chapter/scene with placeholder content, returns IDs for routing
- Uses existing story/scene localStorage patterns

### Phase 2: Core modal and step components

**Step 2.1: Build ProgressBar component**
- Files: `src/components/OnboardingModal/ProgressBar.tsx`, inline CSS or parent module
- Props: `currentStep: number`, `totalSteps: number`
- Render: "Step X of Y" text + segmented progress bar (3 segments, filled up to current step)
- Accessibility: `aria-label="Onboarding progress"`, current step announced via `aria-live="polite"`
- Style: Compact, grayscale tokens, horizontal bar with filled/unfilled segments

**Step 2.2: Build StoryNameStep component**
- File: `src/components/OnboardingModal/StoryNameStep.tsx`
- Props: `storyName: string`, `genre: string`, `onStoryNameChange: (name: string) => void`, `onGenreChange: (genre: string) => void`
- Render: 
  - Input for story name (placeholder: "Untitled Story", autofocus on mount)
  - Genre select or chip group (options: Fiction, Non-Fiction, Fantasy, Sci-Fi, Mystery, Romance, Other)
- Validation: Story name optional (defaults to "Untitled Story"), genre optional
- Style: Vertical layout, generous spacing, grayscale tokens

**Step 2.3: Build ChapterSceneStep component**
- File: `src/components/OnboardingModal/ChapterSceneStep.tsx`
- Props: `chapterTitle: string`, `sceneTitle: string`, `onChapterTitleChange`, `onSceneTitleChange`
- Render:
  - Input for chapter title (placeholder: "Chapter 1")
  - Input for scene title (placeholder: "Opening Scene")
  - Small preview card showing scaffold text (read-only, preview what will appear in editor)
- Validation: Both fields optional (defaults: "Chapter 1", "Opening Scene")
- Style: Vertical layout, preview card with subtle border, muted text

**Step 2.4: Build ObjectiveCard component**
- File: `src/components/OnboardingModal/ObjectiveCard.tsx`
- Props: `title: string`, `description: string`, `selected: boolean`, `onClick: () => void`
- Render: Card with title, 1-2 line description, selected state (border highlight)
- Accessibility: `role="radio"`, keyboard-selectable (Space/Enter), focus visible
- Style: Horizontal layout (desktop) or stacked (mobile), selected card has darker border

**Step 2.5: Build ObjectiveStep component**
- File: `src/components/OnboardingModal/ObjectiveStep.tsx`
- Props: `objective: string | null`, `onObjectiveChange: (obj: string) => void`
- Render: Three ObjectiveCard components (Draft, Revise, Brainstorm) with descriptions:
  - Draft: "Start writing your first draft"
  - Revise: "Edit and improve existing text"
  - Brainstorm: "Explore ideas and outlines"
- Single-select behavior (radio group pattern)
- Validation: Optional (defaults to "Draft" if not selected)
- Style: Horizontal card grid on desktop, vertical stack on mobile

### Phase 3: Main modal container

**Step 3.1: Build OnboardingModal component**
- Files: `src/components/OnboardingModal/OnboardingModal.tsx`, `.module.css`
- Props: `open: boolean`, `onClose: () => void`, `onComplete: (data: OnboardingData) => void`
- State: `currentStep: number`, form data for all three steps
- Render:
  - Modal overlay (fixed position, backdrop)
  - Centered panel (max-width, single column)
  - ProgressBar at top
  - Current step component (StoryNameStep, ChapterSceneStep, or ObjectiveStep)
  - Footer with Back, Continue/Create, Skip buttons
- Navigation:
  - "Continue" advances to next step (validates optional fields, shows defaults)
  - "Back" returns to previous step
  - "Skip onboarding" closes modal, marks skipped
  - Step 3 "Create scene" button calls `onComplete` with form data
- Keyboard: Tab order through inputs and buttons, Escape closes modal (shows skip confirmation)
- Accessibility: `role="dialog"`, `aria-labelledby` for heading, focus trap, restore focus on close
- Style: White background, subtle shadow, grayscale borders, generous padding

**Step 3.2: Wire up step transitions**
- Track form data across steps (state in OnboardingModal)
- Validate each step before advancing (allow empty fields with defaults)
- Track step view/continue/skip events via `trackOnboardingStep`
- On "Create scene": call `onComplete` with all form data, parent handles story creation + routing

### Phase 4: Integration with Stories page

**Step 4.1: Add onboarding trigger to Stories page**
- File: `src/app/stories/page.tsx` (modify)
- Add state: `const [showOnboarding, setShowOnboarding] = useState(false)`
- On mount: If `!hasSeenOnboarding()` and user clicks "Create story", show onboarding modal instead of default flow
- Handle onboarding completion:
  - Call `createStoryWithChapter(data)` to create story/chapter/scene
  - Call `markOnboardingComplete()`
  - Route to `/story/[storyId]` with editor mode
- Handle skip: Call `markOnboardingSkipped()`, allow normal story creation

**Step 4.2: Ensure editor loads with scaffold**
- File: `src/app/story/[id]/page.tsx` (verify existing behavior)
- When routed from onboarding, scene content should contain placeholder text
- Verify cursor focuses in editor textarea on mount
- Breadcrumb shows chapter/scene titles from onboarding

### Phase 5: Loading states and error handling

**Step 5.1: Add loading state for story creation**
- In OnboardingModal: Add `isCreating: boolean` state
- On "Create scene" click: Set `isCreating = true`, show inline spinner with text "Creating your first scene..."
- Disable all form controls and navigation buttons while creating
- On success: Show brief "Your first scene is ready" message (<1s), then route to editor
- On error: Show inline error banner at top of modal, preserve form data, allow retry

**Step 5.2: Handle empty state for users who skip**
- File: `src/app/stories/page.tsx`
- If user skips onboarding and has zero stories, show enhanced empty state:
  - Message: "Ready to start writing? Create your first story."
  - CTA: "Create story" button (bypasses onboarding on subsequent clicks)
- Reassurance text: "You can create chapters and scenes as you write."

### Phase 6: Responsive behavior and polish

**Step 6.1: Mobile responsive layout**
- File: `src/components/OnboardingModal/OnboardingModal.module.css`
- Desktop: Centered modal, max-width ~500px, vertical padding
- Mobile (<768px): Full-height bottom sheet, sticky footer with actions
- All steps: Single-column layout on mobile, no side-by-side inputs
- Objective cards: Stack vertically on mobile instead of horizontal grid

**Step 6.2: Polish and microcopy**
- Step 1 heading: "Start your first story"
- Step 2 heading: "Set up your first scene"
- Step 3 heading: "Choose your writing goal"
- Success message: "Your first scene is ready"
- Skip button text: "Skip onboarding" (not "Skip" alone for clarity)
- Back button: Hidden on step 1, visible on steps 2-3

**Step 6.3: Focus management**
- On modal open: Focus first input (story name)
- On step advance: Focus first input of next step
- On modal close: Restore focus to trigger button on Stories page

## Test plan

**Unit tests (if framework exists):**
- `src/lib/onboarding.ts`: Test `hasSeenOnboarding`, `markOnboardingComplete`, persistence
- Story creation: Test `createStoryWithChapter` creates story + chapter + scene with placeholder

**Integration tests:**
1. First-time user clicks "Create story" → Onboarding modal opens
2. Complete all 3 steps → Story/chapter/scene created → Routes to editor with scaffold
3. Skip onboarding on step 1 → Modal closes, flag set, onboarding not shown again
4. User with existing stories → Onboarding not shown
5. Refresh page after skip → Onboarding stays hidden
6. Complete onboarding, create second story → Onboarding not shown

**Manual testing:**

- **Keyboard accessibility:**
  - Tab through all inputs and buttons in logical order
  - Enter/Space activates buttons and selects objective cards
  - Escape closes modal (with confirmation if in progress)
  - Focus visible on all interactive elements
  - Screen reader announces step progress

- **Responsive:**
  - Desktop: Centered modal, horizontal objective cards
  - Mobile: Full-height sheet, sticky footer, stacked objective cards
  - Modal does not break layout on small screens

- **User flows:**
  - Complete onboarding with all fields filled → Lands in editor with correct titles
  - Complete onboarding with empty fields → Defaults applied (Untitled Story, Chapter 1, Opening Scene)
  - Skip on step 1 → No story created, can create manually
  - Skip on step 2 → Modal closes, onboarding marked skipped
  - Back button navigates to previous step without losing data

- **Edge cases:**
  - Very long story/chapter/scene names (50+ chars): Truncate in preview, full value saved
  - Network error during creation: Error banner shown, retry available
  - User closes tab mid-onboarding: Onboarding state not marked complete, shows again on next visit
  - User has onboarding-completed flag but zero stories: Can still create stories normally

**Commands:**
```bash
npm run dev
# Visit http://localhost:3000/stories
# Clear localStorage key: onboarding-completed
# Click "Create story" → Should show onboarding
# Complete flow, verify lands in editor
# Return to /stories, try creating second story → Onboarding should not appear
```

## Risks and mitigations

**Risk 1: Modal blocks experienced users**
- Problem: Power users forced to skip every time if flag fails
- Impact: Frustration, friction in story creation flow
- Mitigation: Make skip button prominent, one-click action. Persist flag reliably in localStorage. Future: Add user preference toggle in settings.

**Risk 2: Placeholder text feels prescriptive**
- Problem: Users feel locked into suggested structure
- Impact: Discourages experimentation
- Mitigation: Use minimal, neutral placeholder ("Start writing..."). Clearly communicate that titles/text are editable. Future: Offer blank scaffold option.

**Risk 3: Onboarding feels slow compared to direct creation**
- Problem: 3 steps take longer than one-click "New story"
- Impact: Activation improvement offset by time-to-write
- Mitigation: Allow empty fields with smart defaults. Keep each step single-action. Track time-to-completion in analytics to validate <2min target.

**Risk 4: Onboarding state tied to localStorage is fragile**
- Problem: Clearing browser data resets onboarding flag, users see it again
- Impact: Annoyance, perceived bug
- Mitigation: Accept localStorage limitation for v1 (no backend yet). Future: Sync onboarding state to user profile when auth is added.

**Risk 5: Objective selection has no functional impact in v1**
- Problem: Selecting "Draft" vs "Revise" doesn't change editor behavior
- Impact: Feels like false choice, reduces trust
- Mitigation: Keep step 3 lightweight. Future: Use objective to customize editor onboarding tooltips or suggested actions.

## Rollback strategy

**If critical bugs:**
1. Revert PR merge commit
2. Story creation flow returns to pre-onboarding behavior (direct creation)
3. No data loss: Stories/scenes created via onboarding persist normally

**Feature flag (future):**
- Add `ENABLE_ONBOARDING` environment variable
- If false, bypass onboarding modal entirely
- Allows instant rollback without code deploy

**Graceful degradation:**
- If onboarding state helpers fail: Show onboarding every time (annoying but safe)
- If story creation fails: Show error, allow retry or manual creation
- If modal rendering crashes: Wrap in error boundary, fall back to direct story creation

## Observability

**Logs:**
- Console log each onboarding event: "Onboarding: step 1 viewed", "Onboarding: step 2 continued", "Onboarding: completed", "Onboarding: skipped"
- Log story creation: "Created story via onboarding: {storyId}"

**Metrics (future analytics):**
- Track onboarding funnel: Step 1 viewed → Step 2 viewed → Step 3 viewed → Completed
- Track skip rate by step: "Skipped on step X"
- Track time-to-completion (target: <2 minutes)
- Track activation rate: % of users who complete onboarding and write 200+ words in first session

**Event instrumentation points:**
- `onboarding_step_viewed` (step number)
- `onboarding_step_continued` (step number)
- `onboarding_skipped` (step number)
- `onboarding_completed` (time elapsed)
- `first_scene_created` (via onboarding vs. manual)
- `editor_first_200_words` (milestone for activation)

## Acceptance mapping

| Acceptance Criteria | Implementation |
|---------------------|----------------|
| New user is offered onboarding once after first story creation | Stories page checks `hasSeenOnboarding()`, shows modal if false, marks complete/skip on finish |
| User can complete onboarding in under 2 minutes and land in editable first scene | 3 quick steps with defaults → `createStoryWithChapter` → route to editor with focus on textarea |
| User can skip onboarding at any step and still access core editor | "Skip onboarding" button in footer → `markOnboardingSkipped()` → modal closes, normal story creation available |
| Onboarding completion/skip state persists and prevents repeated prompting | localStorage key `onboarding-completed` checked on mount, never shown again after complete/skip |
| Event instrumentation exists for each onboarding step and completion outcome | `trackOnboardingStep` logs each step view/continue/skip action, completion tracked on final step |

## Handoff

**Implementation readiness:**
- ✅ Product requirements reviewed
- ✅ Design brief reviewed (3-step modal, skip controls, story creation flow)
- ✅ Current Stories page and Editor route architecture understood
- ✅ Implementation plan complete with 6 phases

**Code Monkey: Key implementation notes**
1. **Phase order:** State management → Components → Modal → Integration → Loading/errors → Polish
2. **Skip controls:** Must be prominent and one-click (not buried in nested dialogs)
3. **Defaults:** All form fields optional, apply sensible defaults (Untitled Story, Chapter 1, Opening Scene)
4. **localStorage:** Use key `onboarding-completed` with values `"completed"` or `"skipped"`
5. **Focus management:** Autofocus first input on each step, restore focus on modal close
6. **Event instrumentation:** Console.log for v1, structured for future analytics integration
7. **Refer to Design brief** for exact microcopy, step descriptions, and visual hierarchy

Assign to: Code Monkey



</plan>

title: Add chapter and scene navigator with drag-and-drop ordering
description: ## Problem Writers need structure while drafting, but the current editor is a single undifferentiated text area. Without chapters/scenes, users cannot plan pacing, reorder narrative beats, or navigate long manuscripts efficiently. ## Target user Novelists and serial fiction writers managing multi-chapter drafts who need scene-level organization similar to Living Writer clarity, without extra clutter. ## Outcome / KPI - Increase average number of scenes created per active manuscript - Improve navigation efficiency (time to jump to target scene) - Reduce manuscript abandonment after chapter 1 ## Scope - Add left-side manuscript tree: chapters with nested scenes - Support create/edit/delete for chapters and scenes - Drag-and-drop reorder for chapters and scenes (with keyboard-accessible fallback controls) - Editor writes to selected scene, not global document blob - Persist ordering and selection across reloads ## Non-goals - Timeline visualization - Character/worldbuilding ontology - Collaboration features ## Acceptance criteria - User can create chapter + scene and switch between scenes without data loss - Reordering scenes updates persisted order and survives refresh - Scene title updates reflect in navigator and editor header - Deleting a scene requires confirmation and cannot silently remove content - Keyboard users can reorder without mouse-only interactions ## Priority rationale Structured drafting is a core differentiator versus plain document tools and is foundational for future outlining, continuity, and revision intelligence. Shipping this now moves the app from generic editor to story-first product. ## Handoff Assign to: Market Research ## Goal Add a chapter/scene navigator with hierarchical manuscript tree, scene-specific editing, and persistent scene content storage. ## Scope and files **New files (9):** 1. `src/types/chapter.ts` - Chapter type definitions 2. `src/lib/chapters.ts` - Chapter hierarchy CRUD operations 3. `src/components/ManuscriptNavigator/ManuscriptNavigator.tsx` - Main tree navigator 4. `src/components/ManuscriptNavigator/ManuscriptNavigator.module.css` - Navigator styles 5. `src/components/ChapterRow/ChapterRow.tsx` - Collapsible chapter row with nested scenes 6. `src/components/ChapterRow/ChapterRow.module.css` - Chapter row styles 7. `src/components/SceneRow/SceneRow.tsx` - Scene row with drag handle and actions 8. `src/components/SceneRow/SceneRow.module.css` - Scene row styles 9. `src/components/ConfirmDialog/ConfirmDialog.tsx` + `.module.css` - Reusable delete confirmation modal **Modified files (4):** 1. `src/types/scene.ts` -
